text: We call the shots. We call the shots. We call the shots. We call the shots. We call the shots. We call the shots. We filed for a union back in March of twenty twenty two, and had our election back in May. It was an eighteen to zero landslide vote for the union. Starbucks is doubling down on its union busting tactics, so we are out here doubling down on our strikes. Starbucks continues to withhold benefits from its union stores, like credit card tipping, but gives it to non-unionized stores. They also are cutting our hours and targeting union employees by cutting their hours and withholding benefits from them, so they can't receive Starbucks benefits. We're out here together and solidly standing against the company as they make billions of dollars, but as our employees fail to scrape by and struggle to pay their bills, we're out here standing up against the billion-dollar corporation because we're here stronger together, and we're not gonna stop until you know everyone in our stores reinstated. Starbucks has fired six of our workers so far, five of them for cleaning our store. I like to say was fired for bleaching a seat. This was my first job. I started back when I was sixteen, and I was fired ultimately for trying to make it a better workplace. Starbucks over here is bullying. Union busting is bullying, and we're not gonna back down until we get our fair contract. We're gonna get it now! We're gonna get it! We're gonna get it! We're gonna get it! We're gonna get it! We're gonna get it! We're gonna get it! We're gonna get it! We're gonna get it!
